description_long: This object is a commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage, swept-back low-mounted wings, and a conventional tail assembly. The body is predominantly white with blue and gray striping along the fuselage, and the words 'American Airlines' are visible on the side. The aircraft features two engines mounted under the wings, a tricycle landing gear configuration with two main gear units and a nose gear, and a forward-placed, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The wings are swept and lack winglets, and the overall design is typical of mid-to-late 20th-century passenger jets. The surface appears smooth and metallic, with a glossy finish reflecting light.
description: A white and blue commercial jet airliner with swept-back low wings, underwing engines, and a conventional tail, marked 'American Airlines' on the fuselage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the 'American Airlines' logo, and both wings with visible underwing engines.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the swept-back wings, tail assembly, and the two main landing gear units beneath the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage, tail, and wing configuration, with the landing gear and engines visible.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit windows, forward fuselage, and the symmetrical sweep of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted under each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
